items.title: Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
items.summary: Thinking Machines releases Inkling, a 975B-parameter (41B active) open-weights multimodal Mixture-of-Experts model with controllable thinking effort, available for fine-tuning on Tinker.

items.title: Bonsai 27B: Full 27B-Class Reasoning in Binary and Ternary Transformer Weights — On Laptops and Phones

items.title: FlowWAM: Optical Flow as a Unified Action Representation for World Action Models

items.title: Inside TPU and GPU Clusters: The Anatomy of Collective Communication

items.title: Hy-Embodied-VLM-1.0: Efficient Physical-World Agents

items.title: Xiaomi-Robotics-U0: Unified Embodied Synthesis with World Foundation Model
items.summary: Recent foundation image and video generation models offer strong generalization and controllability, but their direct application to embodied scenarios is limited by requirements for multi-view consistency, geometric coherence, and robot embodiment constraints. Existing methods t

items.title: AIDE²: The First Evidence of Recursive Self-Improvement
items.summary: An autoresearch agent pointed at its own harness discovered seven successive improved versions of itself in eight unattended days, beating a baseline hand-tuned over two years on held-out benchmarks it never optimized against.

items.title: Quantum States Prepared by Wormholes: Long-Wavelength Deviations from Bunch-Davies
items.summary: Wineglass wormholes mediate the nucleation of baby universes out of an asymptotically Anti-de Sitter or flat spacetime. Upon materialization, the new universe naturally undergoes an inflationary phase. Here we study the quantum state of tensor and probe scalar field fluctuations

items.title: Qwen-Music Technical Report
items.summary: In this report, we introduce Qwen-Music, a powerful music generation model capable of producing highly musical and high-fidelity songs with complete vocal singing. Qwen-Music supports two core tasks: Text to Music Generation, which create entirely new songs from text descriptions

items.title: WanToFight: Real-Time Generative Game Engine for Multi-Player Combat Interaction
items.summary: We present WanToFight, a generative game engine that simulates real-time, two-player The King of Fighters '97 (KOF~'97) gameplay from keyboard input. Prior generative game engines target either single-player first-person settings or non-real-time cooperative scenarios; multi-play

items.title: Cover Ratios for Hyperscaler Bonds Declining
items.summary: The cover ratio measures how many dollars of investor orders a bond deal receives for every dollar of bonds issued.

items.title: How Exposed Is Your Job to AI? The Experts Can’t Agree
items.summary: To gauge how much AI affects a job, researchers rate it from 0 to 1 based on how many of its tasks could be, or already are, done with AI.

items.title: The Dollar’s Hidden Dependence on the AI Trade
items.summary: Net foreign inflows into US equities have surged to a record high, driven in large part by overseas investors seeking AI exposure they cannot get in their home markets, see chart below.

items.title: Jevons Paradox: More Evidence of a Positive Employment Effect of AI Adoption
items.summary: A new study from Ramp, linking observed AI spending to workforce records across 21,559 US firms, finds that companies adopting AI grow their headcount 10% over the two years following adoption.

items.title: Monitoring the AI Trade: How Growing Debt Issuance Is Reshaping the Landscape
items.summary: Hyperscaler debt issuance continues to grow, reshaping dynamics across financial markets.

items.summary: Syntetica, a French startup that has developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, has already obtained big-name partners and investors.

items.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
items.summary: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

items.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
items.summary: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
items.summary: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

items.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
items.summary: Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person's health.

items.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
items.summary: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.summary: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.summary: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with seve

items.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
items.summary: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.
